title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. smaragdinum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. smaragdinum, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, known for its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. smaragdinum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular remedy for minor cuts and burns. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically consumed in moderation to reap its medicinal benefits.
